name: Rota da Água — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto
name_original: Rota da Água — percurso circular Parque Florestal de Monsanto
region_intro: Jogging around São Domingos De Benfica offers access to extensive green spaces and well-connected parks, making it a suitable location for outdoor activity. The area is characterized by its integration with the expansive Monsanto Forest Park, Lisbon's largest urban green space, providing varied terrain and natural woodlands. Within the parish, areas like Mata de São Domingos de Benfica and Parque do Calhau contribute to a network of pathways and scenic viewpoints. This blend of natural features and urban accessibility supports a range of running experiences. Best jogging routes around São Domingos De Benfica The most popular jogging route is Rota da Água — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 52 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path through the extensive Monsanto Forest Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Route of the Six Quarries — Monsanto Forest Park, a difficult 2.2 miles (3.5 km) path. This trail explores ancient limestone quarries within Monsanto Forest Park. Local runners also love the Campo Grande Lake – Lake in Jardim do Campo Grande loop from Laranjeiras, a 7.2 miles (11.5 km) trail leading through urban green spaces, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Jogging around São Domingos De Benfica is defined by extensive woodlands, urban parks, and connections to the vast Monsanto Forest Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in São Domingos De Benfica are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 45 reviews. More than 1300 runners have used komoot to explore São Domingos De Benfica's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in São Domingos De Benfica?
A: São Domingos De Benfica offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 40 distinct trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy strolls to more challenging runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in São Domingos De Benfica?
A: Runners in São Domingos De Benfica can expect a diverse range of terrain. The area is characterized by extensive woodlands, particularly within the vast Monsanto Forest Park and the local Mata de São Domingos de Benfica. You'll find pathways through urban green spaces, as well as varied natural trails with some elevation changes, offering both shaded forest paths and open parkland.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or casual joggers?
A: Yes, São Domingos De Benfica has routes suitable for beginners. While many trails are moderate, there are at least two easy routes available. The area's numerous parks and green spaces, such as Mata de São Domingos de Benfica, offer gentler pathways perfect for a relaxed jog.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, São Domingos De Benfica provides several difficult routes. There are 18 challenging trails, often featuring more significant elevation changes and varied terrain, especially within the Monsanto Forest Park. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381172387/route-of-the-six-quarries-monsanto-forest-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Six Quarries — Monsanto Forest Park&lt;/a&gt;, which explores ancient limestone quarries.

Q: Are there circular running routes in São Domingos De Benfica?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in São Domingos De Benfica are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381162361/rota-da-agua-circular-route-parque-florestal-de-monsanto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rota da Água — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 4.7-mile (7.5 km) loop through the extensive Monsanto Forest Park.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in São Domingos De Benfica?
A: The running routes in São Domingos De Benfica are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 45 reviews. More than 1300 runners have explored these trails, often praising the extensive green spaces, varied terrain, and the refreshing connection to nature provided by Monsanto Forest Park.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running trails in São Domingos De Benfica?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic viewpoints. Parque do Calhau is home to the Miradouro do Moinho das Três Cruzes, which provides elevated views. Additionally, the expansive Monsanto Forest Park, accessible from São Domingos De Benfica, features various spots with panoramic vistas over Lisbon and the surrounding natural landscape, making for rewarding breaks during your run.

Q: Are the running routes in São Domingos De Benfica suitable for families?
A: Many of the green spaces in São Domingos De Benfica are family-friendly. Areas like Mata de São Domingos de Benfica feature pathways, leisure equipment, and playgrounds, making them suitable for families who want to combine a jog with other outdoor activities. The gentler paths in these parks are ideal for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: São Domingos De Benfica's numerous green spaces and parks, including sections of Monsanto Forest Park, are generally welcoming to dogs. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain the natural beauty and cleanliness of the trails for everyone.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions near the running routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can find interesting landmarks nearby. For instance, the historic Palácio dos Marqueses de Fronteira with its beautiful gardens is close by. You might also encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/853498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;University of Lisbon Botanical Garden&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Christ the King (Santuário de Cristo Rei)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views, though it's a bit further afield.

Q: How accessible are the running trails by public transport?
A: São Domingos De Benfica is well-served by public transportation, including metro access, making its green spaces and connections to Monsanto Forest Park easily reachable. This good connectivity means you can often get to the starting points of many running routes without needing a car.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for endurance training?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, the area offers several options. The connection to the vast Monsanto Forest Park provides opportunities for extended distances. An example of a longer route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32246117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo Grande Lake – Lake in Jardim do Campo Grande loop from Laranjeiras&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 7.2 miles (11.5 km) and takes over an hour to complete.
tips: This point in Monsanto Park offers an unpretentious view of Lisbon. It becomes convenient as a stopping point in the middle of the race.

Parque Eduardo VII
This famous park is named after British King Edward VII who visited the city in the twentieth century. Parque Eduardo VII is the largest park in the center of Lisbon. The park is built against a hill.

The Monsanto Interpretation Center (CIM) is the ideal gateway to an in-depth exploration of the Monsanto Forest Park, Lisbon's largest green space. This center offers a unique educational and environmental experience, combining nature, culture, and sustainability.
